title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Is Death by Heart Attack Covered by Term Insurance?
Heart-related diseases are becoming a rising concern in India, and deaths due to heart attacks have become a common occurrence. American research pointed out a 13% jump in sudden heart attacks among individuals aged in their mid-30s to mid-40s. Meanwhile, the Indian Heart Association stated that heart diseases tend to strike at earlier ages in India compared to other countries1.
The Indian Heart Association backed its claim stating that 50% of heart attacks in Indian men occurred under 50 years of age and 25% occurred under 40 years of age2.
These numbers paint a distressing figure when it comes to deaths due to heart attacks. However, facts are facts. Heart attacks are sudden, and if the breadwinner succumbs to death due to the same, the family incurs considerable financial losses. That is where a term insurance policy comes into picture, as it can provide financial security to the affected family. But is heart attack covered under term insurance?
Let’s find out.
Deaths covered under term insurance
Usually, a term insurance plan covers almost all types of deaths. You get coverage for –
Natural deaths
Accidental deaths
Illness-related deaths
Suicidal deaths (only after 12 months of policy issuance or revival and is subject to policy terms and conditions).
Is heart attack covered under term insurance?
A heart attack is a medical condition that can cause death. Since term plans cover illness-related deaths, a heart attack is covered under the plan.
So, if you are a heart patient and you invest in a term insurance policy, you would be covered for any subsequent heart attack that might result in death during the policy tenure. However, there are some terms & conditions to be followed based on your heart health history.
Factors to be considered when buying a term life insurance plan
While a term insurance plan does cover heart attacks, if you are an existing heart patient, here are some factors that you should consider before you buy the coverage –
Declare your heart condition when you are buying the term life insurance plan. Detail the condition, its history, current medication, etc., completely and correctly.
The insurance company might require you to undergo a pre-entrance medical test before issuing the coverage. This test determines your current heart health based on which the insurer offers coverage.
Your premium might be increased due to the additional risk of covering your heart condition.
The insurance company might also put a restriction on the coverage amount that you can avail of depending on the severity of your heart problem or may reject your application.
If you apply with another insurance company after your application for insurance policy being rejected by the first, disclose the rejection. The second insurer should know that your initial proposal was rejected by the first insurer.
Life insurance plans are contracts of utmost good faith. You are required to disclose all relevant information correctly and completely for a valid policy. If fearing an increase in the premium or a coverage restriction, you hide your heart condition and subsequently suffer a heart attack, the insurance company has the right to nullify your policy and may repudiate the claim on the grounds of breach of good faith.
So, even when you suffer from a heart condition, disclose it outright when buying the policy.
Moreover, it is preferred that you invest in a term plan at a younger age when you are more likely to be free from health-related issues. As you buy the plan young, you might get the following benefits –
The premium would be affordable.
You would be allowed to opt for a suitable sum assured.
There might be no premium loadings due to medical reasons.
Thus, you would be able to opt for comprehensive coverage at affordable premiums when you buy at an early age. If you suffer a heart attack during the policy tenure that causes premature demise, the insurance company will pay the death benefit if the terms & conditions of the policy are met.
The bottom line
Term insurance plans protect against the financial repercussions of premature deaths, whether such deaths are accidental or sudden due to a heart attack. A heart attack is covered under the scope of the policy, and if the contingency strikes, the term plan will compensate your family for the financial loss that they might suffer.
If you are buying the term plan with a heart condition, keep the aforementioned points in mind to ensure optimal coverage and claim settlement. If you are buying the plan in the prime of your health, you may enjoy an all-around scope of protection and get financial security of policy benefits against unforeseen heart attacks.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
